43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
88	
89	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
90	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
91	
92	\begin{definition}
93	
94	\leanok
95	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
96	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
97	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
98	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let \(V\) and \(W\)
99	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
100	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
101	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi\) is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
102	  % \(v\) of \(V\) if \(v \in U\) for some \((U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi\). The set \(U_1\) of
103	  % \(v\) at which \(\varphi\) is defined is open, and there is a regular map
104	  % \(\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W\) such that \((U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi\) --- clearly,
105	  % \(U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U\) and we can define \(\varphi_1\) to be
106	  % the regular map such that \(\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U\) for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
107	  % \varphi$."
108	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
109	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
110	  The \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\) is the open set
111	  \[
112	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
113	  \]
114	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The
115	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of \(f\) is the closed complement
116	  \[
117	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
118	  \]
119	  The map \(f\) is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset\).
120	
121	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
122	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} is a method on the
123	  Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure pinned at
124	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; it returns a
125	  \texttt{Set X} (the underlying carrier of the closed complement of
126	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain}, which is already exposed by Mathlib). The
127	  closedness of \(Z(f)\) is a separate \texttt{IsClosed} witness, packaged either as a
128	  field of \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} or as a standalone lemma
129	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus}, whichever is cleaner with
130	  Mathlib's existing API at commit \texttt{b80f227}.
131	\end{definition}
132	
133	\begin{definition}
134	
135	\leanok
136	  [Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps]
137	  \label{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}
138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree}
139	  
140	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 --- the conclusion
141	  % "an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)"
142	  % is the standing predicate we abstract (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf,
143	  % PDF page 22).
144	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow W\) from a normal
145	  % variety \(V\) to a complete variety \(W\) is defined on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose
146	  % complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)."
147	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 (the
148	  conclusion).}
149	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
150	  We say \(f\) is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-\(\geq 2\)
151	  indeterminacy}) if every irreducible component of the closed subset \(Z(f) \subseteq X\)
152	  has codimension \(\geq 2\) in \(X\). Equivalently, no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in
153	  \(Z(f)\); equivalently, every point \(\eta \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\)
154	  lies in \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).
155	
156	  \paragraph{Why the abstraction.} Milne's Theorem~3.1 (\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}
157	  below) and Lemma~3.3 (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} below) both produce
158	  conclusions about the codimension of \(Z(f)\). Phrasing the codim-\(\geq 2\) condition
159	  as a named predicate keeps the cited statements concise and lets the consumer
160	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} read off the combination
161	  (\(Z(f)\) has codim \(\geq 2\) from \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} \emph{and} \(Z(f)\) is
162	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} \(\Rightarrow\) \(Z(f)\)
163	  is empty) as a single line.
164	
165	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
166	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} is a \texttt{Prop}-valued
167	  predicate, encoded via Mathlib's \texttt{Order.coheight} on the specialisation
168	  preorder of \texttt{X.carrier} as in
169	  \cref{def:prime_divisor}: \(f\) is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if and only if for every
170	  \(\eta \in X\) with \(\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1\), $\eta \in
171	  \texttt{f.domain}\(. The encoding matches the standing \)\mathrm{Order.coheight}$
172	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of \(X\) and the
173	  hypothesis ``no codim-\(1\) indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.
174	\end{definition}
175	
176	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
177	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
178	
179	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
180	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
181	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
182	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
183	
184	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
185	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
186	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
187	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
188	
189	\subsection{Stages 5a/5b: K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate (iter-193)}
190	\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}
191	
192	The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
193	(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) factors on the Lean side
194	through a six-stage chain. Stages~1--2 (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) flat at a
195	stalk; smooth \(\Rightarrow\) admits a standard-smooth presentation
196	locally) are axiom-clean since iter-191; Stages~3--4 (the
197	scheme-to-algebra bridge for standard-smooth presentations; freeness of
198	\(\Omega_{S/R}\) for a standard-smooth algebra \(R \to S\)) are
199	axiom-clean since iter-192. The remaining Stages~5--6 reduce the
200	Stacks~00TT conclusion to a freeness plus rank computation on K\"ahler
201	differentials, transported through localisation at the prime defining
202	the stalk. Iter-193 packaged Stages~5a (transport of freeness) and 5b
203	(matching rank under localisation) as the two standalone helpers below;
204	Stage~6 (the remaining Stacks~00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula
205	plus Stacks~02JK cotangent--K\"ahler-over-a-field bridge) is the open
206	Mathlib gap, decomposed iter-198 into the three named sub-lemmas
207	\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (6.A),
208	\cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (6.B), and
209	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} (6.C) in
210	\cref{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition} immediately following.
211	
212	\begin{lemma}
213	
214	\leanok
215	  [Stage 5a --- K\"ahler-differentials freeness transports through localisation]
216	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
217	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization}
218	  Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings equipped with an \(R\)-algebra
219	  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
220	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
221	  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that
222	  \(R \to S \to S_M\) is a tower of \(R\)-algebras. Then the module of
223	  K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) is free as an
224	  \(S_M\)-module.
225	\end{lemma}
226	
227	\begin{proof}
228	  The canonical comparison map
229	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) induced by the localisation map
230	  \(S \to S_M\) realises \(\Omega_{S_M/R}\) as the localisation of the
231	  \(S\)-module \(\Omega_{S/R}\) at \(M\); i.e.\ it satisfies Mathlib's
232	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} predicate along the underlying ring
233	  localisation. This is Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, packaged in Mathlib as
234	  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map}. Freeness of a
235	  module is preserved under localisation of modules via Mathlib's
236	  \texttt{Module.free\_of\_isLocalizedModule}; combining the two yields
237	  the conclusion.
238	\end{proof}
239	
240	\begin{lemma}
241	
242	\leanok
243	  [Stage 5b --- K\"ahler-differentials rank under localisation equals
244	  the relative dimension]
245	  \label{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
246	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
247	  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
248	  With notation as in \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
249	  assume in addition that the \(R\)-algebra \(S\) is \emph{standard
250	  smooth of relative dimension \(n\)} (Mathlib's
251	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,\(n\)\,\(R\)\,\(S\),
252	  the algebra-side packaging of Stacks tag \texttt{00T7}(2)) and that
253	  the localisation \(S_M\) is non-trivial as a ring. Then the rank of
254	  \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) over \(S_M\) equals \(n\).
255	\end{lemma}
256	
257	\begin{proof}
258	  Mathlib's
259	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak rank\_kaehlerDifferential}
260	  asserts \(\mathrm{rank}_S(\Omega_{S/R}) = n\) under the
261	  standard-smooth hypothesis. The localisation map
262	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) is an
263	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} along \(S \to S_M\) (Stacks tag
264	  \texttt{02JK}; cf.\ the proof of
265	  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}), and Mathlib's
266	  \texttt{Module.lift\_rank\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_free} transports
267	  module rank through an \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} up to
268	  \(\mathrm{Cardinal.lift}\). Since \(n\) is a natural number both
269	  ranks equal \(n\), so
270	  \(\mathrm{rank}_{S_M}(\Omega_{S_M / R}) = n\) as required.
271	\end{proof}
272	
273	\subsection{Stage 6 sub-decomposition: 00OE + 02JK chain (iter-198)}
274	\label{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}
275	
276	Stage 6 is the residual gap inside
277	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}'s six-stage chain: given Stages
278	1--5b (a standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart
279	\(R \to S\) at a point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\)
280	and \(\Omega_{S_\mathfrak q / R}\) free of rank \(n\) on the stalk
281	\(S_\mathfrak q = \mathcal O_{X, z}\)), one must conclude that
282	\(S_\mathfrak q\) is a regular local ring. The Stacks proof of tag
283	\texttt{00TT} ultimately reduces to two ingredients which Mathlib does
284	not yet ship at commit \texttt{b80f227}: (6.A) the smooth-algebra
285	Krull-dimension formula (Stacks tag \texttt{00OE}, in the algebra
286	chapter as Lemma~\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}),
287	and (6.B) the cotangent / K\"ahler-differential bridge over a field
288	base (Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, in the algebra chapter as
289	Lemma~\texttt{lemma-differential-seq} plus the separable-residue-field
290	refinement of Lemma~\texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}). The two
291	ingredients combine (6.C) into the regularity conclusion via
292	\(\dim S_\mathfrak q = \mathrm{rank}_{S_\mathfrak q}\Omega_{S_\mathfrak
293	q / R} = \dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\),
294	which is the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity that
295	Mathlib's \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.iff\_finrank\_cotangentSpace}
296	already exposes. The next three lemmas package the three sub-gaps as
297	named iter-199+ prover targets.
298	
299	\begin{lemma}
300	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
301	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
302	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
303	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
304	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
305	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
306	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
307	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
308	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
309	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
310	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
311	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
312	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
313	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
314	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
315	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
316	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
317	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
318	  \[
319	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
320	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
321	  \]
322	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
323	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
324	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
325	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
326	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
327	
328	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the iter-193 Stage~3
329	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
330	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) (Stage
331	  3), and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
332	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
333	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
334	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
335	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
336	\end{lemma}
337	
338	\begin{lemma}
339	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
340	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
341	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
342	  % \lean{...} updated iter-199 review: iter-199 prover landed the
343	  % closed-point iso as a project-local axiom-clean helper (private
344	  % theorem in AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme namespace). Companion siblings:
345	  %   cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
346	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
347	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue (closed-point bundled)
348	  \uses{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
349	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-differential-seq (canonically
350	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
351	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
352	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
353	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
354	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\ref{equation-functorial-omega}),
355	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
356	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
357	  % $S'$-modules
358	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
359	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
360	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
361	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
362	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
363	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
364	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
365	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
366	  % (see Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
367	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
369	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
370	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
371	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
372	  \[
373	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
374	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
375	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
376	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
377	  \]
378	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
379	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
380	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
381	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
382	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
383	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
384	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
385	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
386	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
387	  \[
388	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
389	  \]
390	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
391	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
392	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
393	\end{lemma}
394	
395	\begin{lemma}
396	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
397	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
398	  % NOTE (iter-199): the Lean target for this 6.C assembly is the body
399	  % of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}
400	  % itself (see \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}); the iter-198
401	  % blueprint named a planned \texttt{...\_aux} extraction that has not
402	  % been created in the Lean file. Treat the lemma below as the in-body
403	  % closure pattern that the existing helper consumes once Stages 6.A and
404	  % 6.B land, rather than as a separately-pinned Lean declaration.
405	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
406	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
407	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
408	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
409	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
410	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
411	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
412	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
413	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
414	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
415	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
416	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
417	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
418	  dimension equals \(1\).
419	\end{lemma}
420	
421	\begin{proof}
422	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
423	  \begin{enumerate}
424	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
425	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
426	      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
427	    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
428	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
429	      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
430	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
431	      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
432	      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
433	      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
434	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
435	      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
436	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
437	      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
438	      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
439	      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
440	      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
441	      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
442	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
443	      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
444	    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
445	      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
446	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
447	      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
448	      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
449	      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
450	      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
451	      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
452	      regular. The scheme-level transfer
453	      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3
454	      \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} on the affine chart) completes
455	      the conclusion.
456	  \end{enumerate}
457	\end{proof}

503	    \texttt{IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} \(\leftrightarrow\)
504	    \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} bridge is not assembled. NEEDS-BRIDGE;
505	    project build of \(\sim\)100--200~LOC.
506	  \item \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.iff\_finrank\_cotangentSpace} ---
507	    EXISTS (\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/RegularLocal/Basic.lean}); this
508	    is the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity that
509	    Stage 6.C feeds into. Consumes \texttt{Module.finrank} of
510	    \texttt{IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} \(=\) \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m
511	    / \mathfrak m^2\).
512	  \item \texttt{Algebra.IsSmooth.krullDim\_stalk} or any direct
513	    smooth-algebra \(\Rightarrow\) regular-stalk shortcut --- MISSING
514	    at \texttt{b80f227}. The standard scheme-level packaging of Stacks
515	    00TT is not in Mathlib; the iter-191--193 staged decomposition is
516	    the project-side substitute.
517	  \item \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.localization\_isRegularLocalRing}
518	    (preservation of regularity under localisation at a prime;
519	    Stacks \texttt{tag 00OF}) --- EXISTS; used as the
520	    closed-point-to-arbitrary-point promotion step once Stage 6.C is
521	    in scope.
522	\end{itemize}
523	
524	\paragraph{Cascade to consumers.} Closure of Stage 6 (i.e.\ landing
525	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} axiom-clean) collapses the
526	following residual sorries in
527	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean}:
528	\begin{itemize}
529	  \item \texttt{isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth} (L434--L526) ---
530	    its final \texttt{sorry} on L526 is filled by chaining the Stage 5a/5b
531	    helpers with the new Stage 6.A/6.B/6.C bridges, exactly as the
532	    proof of \cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} sketches. Closure
533	    is automatic once the three sub-lemmas land axiom-clean.
534	  \item \texttt{smooth\_codim\_one\_maximalIdeal\_isPrincipal\_and\_ne\_bot}
535	    (L547--L599) --- already axiom-clean modulo the named Stacks-00TT
536	    gap; the regularity half is delegated to
537	    \texttt{isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}, so closure
538	    propagates upward without further work.
539	  \item \texttt{localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} (L615--L644) ---
540	    likewise upgrades from gated to axiom-clean automatically: it
541	    consumes \texttt{smooth\_codim\_one\_maximalIdeal\_isPrincipal\_and\_ne\_bot}
542	    and then closes via \texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE}.
543	  \item \texttt{extend\_of\_codimOneFree\_of\_smooth} (the body
544	    around L703--L723) --- Step~1 of
545	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s proof consumes
546	    \texttt{localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} via the valuative
547	    criterion at codim-\(1\) points, so Stage 6 closure unblocks
548	    Step~1 entirely. Step~2 (the depth-\(\geq 2\) local-cohomology
549	    vanishing, Stacks tag \texttt{0AVF}) remains an independent
550	    Mathlib gap and is \emph{not} unblocked by Stage 6.
551	  \item \texttt{indeterminacy\_pure\_codim\_one\_into\_grpScheme}
552	    (L752--L798, the Lean body of
553	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}) --- not directly gated on

555	    pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma, Sub-step~4 of

560	
561	The two remaining post-Stage-6 gaps in the chapter are therefore:
562	(a) the Step~2 depth-\(\geq 2\) local-cohomology vanishing
563	(Stacks~\texttt{0AVF}) consumed only by
564	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}, and (b) the
565	function-field-pullback machinery for \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap}
566	consumed by \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} and Sub-step~3 of
567	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}. Neither overlaps with the
568	Stage 6 work, so the three sub-lemmas above are a self-contained

571	\subsection{Stage 6.A iter-200 substrate: Stacks 00OE 3-step decomposition}
572	\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}
573	\textit{Source: \texttt{coe-stacks00oe} mathlib-analogist
574	cross-domain-inspiration recipe (iter-200, persistent
575	\texttt{analogies/coe-stacks00oe.md}); 7 axiom-clean
576	\texttt{private} substrate theorems landed in
577	\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L688--L800 under
578	\texttt{namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme}.}
579	
580	The iter-200 Lane COE prover phase decomposed Stage 6.A
581	(\ref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}, Stacks 00OE) into a
582	3-step Mathlib-chain. Steps 1 and 2 landed axiom-clean as
583	project-local \texttt{private} theorems; Step 3 landed in an
584	additive parameterised form awaiting the Jacobian-regular-sequence
585	witness for the relations of the smooth-algebra presentation.
586	
587	\paragraph{Step 1 --- Krull-dim \(\Rightarrow\) height (trivial,
588	\(\sim 1\) line).} Re-export of Mathlib's
589	\(\texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height}\) under
590	the project-local name
591	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_eq\_height\_atPrime}\); reduces
592	the localised Krull-dim goal to a height-of-maximal-ideal goal.
593	
594	\paragraph{Step 2 --- Polynomial-ring maximal-ideal height
595	(\(\sim 60\) LOC).} For \(\texttt{MvPolynomial}\,(\texttt{Fin}\,n)\,k\)
596	with \(k\) a field, every maximal ideal has height \emph{exactly}
597	\(n\). Lower bound:
598	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_ge\_card\_of\_field}\),
599	induction on \texttt{Fin n} via \(\texttt{MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv}\)
600	+ Mathlib's \(\texttt{Polynomial.height\_eq\_height\_add\_one}\) plus
601	the Jacobson contraction
602	\(\texttt{Polynomial.isMaximal\_comap\_C\_of\_isJacobsonRing}\). Upper
603	bound:
604	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_le\_natCard\_of\_field}\)
605	via \(\texttt{Ideal.height\_le\_ringKrullDim\_of\_ne\_top}\) +
606	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim\_of\_isNoetherianRing}\) +
607	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_eq\_zero\_of\_field}\). Combined: the
608	\texttt{Fin n}-form
609	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_eq\_card}\) and the
610	general \texttt{[Finite ι]}-form
611	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_eq\_natCard}\) via
612	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.renameEquiv}\) +
613	\(\texttt{RingEquiv.height\_map}\). Capstone packaging the
614	Steps 1+2 composition for any localisation at a maximal ideal:
615	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_atMaximal\_MvPolynomial}\).
616	
617	\paragraph{Step 3 --- Drop by regular-sequence length (additive form
618	in hand, witness pending).} Step 3 generally:
619	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim}\,(R'_{m'} / (f_j)_{j \in \sigma}) +
620	 |\sigma| = \texttt{ringKrullDim}\,R'_{m'}\). The additive form is
621	landed as
622	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
623	via Mathlib's
624	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_add\_length\_eq\_ringKrullDim\_of\_isRegular}\),
625	parameterised over a hypothesis
626	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\,R'_{m'}\,\texttt{rs}\)
627	that is the iter-201+ residual substrate obligation. Note:
628	\(\texttt{WithBot}\,\mathbb N_\infty\) carries no \texttt{HSub}
629	instance, so the additive form is the natural API; a subtraction
630	form is not available.
631	
632	\paragraph{Iter-201+ Jacobian-regular-sequence witness recipe.} The
633	residual substrate of Stage 6.A is the Jacobian-criterion
634	regular-sequence witness for the relations of an
635	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\).
636	
637	\textbf{API choice} (per iter-201 \texttt{progress-critic route201}
638	flag): the project-local target predicate is Mathlib's
639	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\), \emph{not}
640	\(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\). Reason: the iter-200 substrate
641	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
642	(\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L807) consumes the strong predicate
643	\(\texttt{IsRegular}\) (via Mathlib's
644	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_add\_length\_eq\_ringKrullDim\_of\_isRegular}\));
645	the localised stalk \(R'_{m'}\) is local, so by Mathlib's
646	\(\texttt{IsLocalRing.isRegular\_iff\_isWeaklyRegular\_of\_subset\_maximalIdeal}\)
647	the two predicates are equivalent under the standing hypothesis that
648	the relations lie in the maximal ideal --- which holds because
649	the Jacobian-criterion conclusion is precisely that the relations
650	generate (a subset of) the maximal ideal at the closed point. The
651	recipe builds \(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\) first (it is the
652	naturally-arising predicate from the Koszul-complex argument) and
653	then promotes to \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) via the bridge lemma; the
654	final consumer call uses the \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) form.
655	
656	The recipe (per the iter-200 prover handoff + the
657	\texttt{coe-stacks00oe} analogist + iter-201 \texttt{coe-stacks00sw}
658	analogist):
659	(i) extract the explicit
660	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\,\bar k\,
661	 \Gamma(X.\texttt{left}, V)\) from
662	\(\texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\) (Stage 6 sub-gap
663	(i) substrate iter-198); (ii) cite Stacks 00SW / 00OW + Matsumura
664	Thm 14.2: the relations \((f_j)_{j \in \sigma}\) of a submersive
665	presentation form a Koszul-regular sequence at the closed point
666	because the Jacobian matrix is invertible modulo the maximal ideal
667	\(\Leftrightarrow\) the images of \(f_j\) in \(\mathfrak m_A /
668	\mathfrak m_A^2\) are \(\kappa\)-linearly independent
669	\(\Rightarrow\) (Matsumura 14.2 / Stacks 00NQ on a regular local
670	ring) the \(f_j\) form a regular sequence in \(A\); (iii) bundle
671	as a 3-step chain:
672	\begin{itemize}
673	  \item \textbf{A1 — Matsumura helper} (\(\sim 30\)--\(50\) LOC,
674	    Mathlib upstream PR candidate): on a regular local Noetherian
675	    ring \((A, \mathfrak m)\) of Krull dim \(n\), a sequence
676	    \(f_1, \dots, f_c \in \mathfrak m\) whose images in
677	    \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) are \(\kappa\)-linearly
678	    independent forms an \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) sequence in \(A\).
679	    Proof: induction on \(c\), dim-drops-by-one via Mathlib's
680	    \(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_span\_singleton\_succ\_eq\_ringKrullDim}\)
681	    (in \texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/KrullDimension/Regular.lean}),
682	    \(\texttt{IsSMulRegular}\) on the non-zero-divisor \(f_1\) via
683	    \(\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing} \Rightarrow \texttt{IsDomain}\) +
684	    \(f_1 \neq 0\) (since \(f_1 \notin \mathfrak m^2\)), then
685	    \(\texttt{isRegular\_cons\_iff}\) to descend.
686	  \item \textbf{A2 — Cotangent-localisation transport}
687	    (\(\sim 20\)--\(30\) LOC): transport
688	    \(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent}\)'s
689	    linear-independence statement from \(I/I^2\) over \(S\) to
690	    \(\mathfrak m_A / \mathfrak m_A^2\) over \(\kappa(\mathfrak m_A)\)
691	    on the localisation \(A = P.\texttt{Ring}_{m'}\). Uses
692	    \(\texttt{LinearIndependent.of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_isRegular}\)
693	    (in \texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/Module.lean}) +
694	    the conormal-localisation iso
695	    \((I/I^2) \otimes_S S_{\mathfrak m} \simeq
696	     (I \cdot A) / (I \cdot A)^2\).
697	  \item \textbf{A3 — Assembly} (\(\sim 10\)--\(15\) LOC): invoke
698	    A1 with the lin-indep premise from A2, then close via
699	    \(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular.isRegular\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_mem}\)
700	    (in \texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/Regular/Flat.lean:65--73} ---
701	    DIRECT INVOCATION). Final declaration:
702	    \(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.relations\_isRegular\_in\_localization}\).
703	\end{itemize}
704	consuming Mathlib's existing
705	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.jacobian\_isUnit}\) +
706	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent}\) +
707	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.cotangentEquiv}\) +
708	\(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular.isRegular\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_mem}\) +
709	\(\texttt{LinearIndependent.of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_isRegular}\) +
710	\(\texttt{IsLocalRing.isRegular\_iff\_isWeaklyRegular\_of\_subset\_maximalIdeal}\);
711	(iv) plug into \texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}
712	+ \texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_atMaximal\_MvPolynomial} for
713	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim}\,S_{m} = n\) (with the standard-smooth
714	presentation's \texttt{relativeDimension} witness as \(n\)). Estimated
715	project-side cost: \(\sim 30-60\) LOC for (ii)+(iii); the full L1061

12	/-!
13	# `RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular` is stable under flat base change
14	
15	## Main results
16	* `RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular.of_flat_of_isBaseChange`: Let `R` be a commutative ring,
17	  `M` be an `R`-module, `S` be a flat `R`-algebra, `N` be the base change of `M` to `S`.
18	  If `[r₁, …, rₙ]` is a weakly regular `M`-sequence, then its image in `N` is a weakly regular
19	  `N`-sequence.
20	-/
21	
22	public section
23	
24	namespace RingTheory.Sequence
25	
26	open Module
27	
28	variable {R S M N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
29	  [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module S N] [IsScalarTower R S N]
30	
31	/-- Let `R` be a commutative ring, `M` be an `R`-module, `S` be a flat `R`-algebra, `N` be the base
32	  change of `M` to `S`. If `[r₁, …, rₙ]` is a weakly regular `M`-sequence, then its image in `N` is
33	  a weakly regular `N`-sequence. -/
34	theorem IsWeaklyRegular.of_flat_of_isBaseChange [Flat R S] {f : M →ₗ[R] N} (hf : IsBaseChange S f)
35	    {rs : List R} (reg : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) : IsWeaklyRegular N (rs.map (algebraMap R S)) := by
36	  induction rs generalizing M N with
37	  | nil => simp
38	  | cons x _ ih =>
39	    simp only [List.map_cons, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff] at reg ⊢
40	    have e := (QuotSMulTop.algebraMapTensorEquivTensorQuotSMulTop x M S).symm ≪≫ₗ
41	      QuotSMulTop.congr ((algebraMap R S) x) hf.equiv
42	    have hg : IsBaseChange S <|
43	        e.toLinearMap.restrictScalars R ∘ₗ TensorProduct.mk R S (QuotSMulTop x M) 1 :=
44	      IsBaseChange.of_equiv e (fun _ ↦ by simp)

48	    have hj : (annihilator R M).jacobson ≤ p.last.1.1 :=
49	      sInf_le ⟨mem_support_iff_of_finite.mp p.last.2, inferInstance⟩
50	    exact (Nat.cast_le.mpr hle).trans <| this _ (hj h)
51	  -- `q` is a chain of primes such that `x ∈ q 1`, `p.length = q.length` and `p.head = q.head`.
52	  obtain ⟨q, hxq, hq, h0, _⟩ : ∃ q : LTSeries (PrimeSpectrum R), _ ∧ _ ∧ p.head = q.head ∧ _ :=
53	    exist_ltSeries_mem_one_of_mem_last (p.map Subtype.val (fun ⦃_ _⦄ lt ↦ lt)) hxp

57	        Submodule.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr <|
58	          (Submodule.top_ne_pointwise_smul_of_mem_jacobson_annihilator h).symm
59	    rw [hp0, ← WithBot.coe_unbot (supportDim R (QuotSMulTop x M)) hb]
60	    exact WithBot.coe_le_coe.mpr (zero_le ((supportDim R (QuotSMulTop x M)).unbot hb + 1))
61	  -- Let `q' i := q (i + 1)`, then `q'` is a chain of prime ideals in `Supp(M/xM)`.
62	  let q' : LTSeries (support R (QuotSMulTop x M)) := {
63	    length := p.length - 1
64	    toFun := by
65	      intro ⟨i, hi⟩
66	      have hi : i + 1 < q.length + 1 :=

72	  }
73	  grw [le_tsub_add (b := p.length) (a := 1), Nat.cast_add_one, supportDim, Order.krullDim,

6	module

8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.SpanRankOperations
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.Basic

11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Field

13	
14	/-!
15	# Regular local rings
16	
17	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, we define `IsRegularLocalRing` as
18	`(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`. This definition is equivalent to
19	the dimension of the cotangent space over the residue field being equal to `ringKrullDim R`,
20	(see `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
21	
22	# Main Definition and Results
23	
24	* `IsRegularLocalRing` : A Noetherian local ring is regular if
25	  `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`,
26	  i.e. its maximal ideal can be generated by `dim R` elements.
27	
28	* `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` : the equivalence of `IsRegularLocalRing` and
29	  `Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R`
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	open IsLocalRing
36	
37	/-- A Noetherian local ring is said to be regular if its maximal ideal
38	can be generated by `dim R` elements. -/
39	@[stacks 00KU]
40	class IsRegularLocalRing (R : Type*) [CommRing R] : Prop extends
41	    IsLocalRing R, IsNoetherianRing R where
42	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R
43	
44	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
45	
46	lemma isRegularLocalRing_iff [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
47	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R :=

279	/-- Weakly regular sequences can be inductively characterized by:
280	* The empty sequence is weakly regular on any module.
281	* If `r` is regular on `M` and `rs` is a weakly regular sequence on `M⧸rM` then
282	  the sequence obtained from `rs` by prepending `r` is weakly regular on `M`.
283	
284	This is the induction principle produced by the inductive definition above.
285	The motive will usually be valued in `Prop`, but `Sort*` works too. -/
286	@[induction_eliminator]
287	def recIterModByRegular
288	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) →
289	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs → Sort*}
290	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive M [] (nil R M))
291	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
292	      (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
293	      (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) →
294	      (ih : motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs h2) → motive M (r :: rs) (cons h1 h2)) :
295	    {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → {rs : List R} →
296	    (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) → motive M rs h
297	  | M, _, _, [], _ => nil M
298	  | M, _, _, r :: rs, h =>
299	    let ⟨h1, h2⟩ := (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mp h
300	    cons r rs h1 h2 (recIterModByRegular nil cons h2)
301	
302	/-- A simplified version of `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular` where the
303	motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsWeaklyRegular`. -/
304	def ndrecIterModByRegular
305	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
306	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive M [])
307	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
308	      (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r → IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs →
309	      motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs → motive M (r :: rs))
310	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
311	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs → motive M rs :=
312	  recIterModByRegular (motive := fun M _ _ rs _ => motive M rs) nil cons
313	
314	/-- An alternate induction principle from `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular`
315	where we mod out by successive elements in both the module and the base ring.
316	This is useful for propagating certain properties of the initial `M`, e.g.
317	faithfulness or freeness, throughout the induction. -/
318	def recIterModByRegularWithRing
319	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →

550	    {rs : List R} {f₁ : M →ₗ[R] M₂} {f₂ : M₂ →ₗ[R] M₃} {f₃ : M₃ →ₗ[R] M₄}
551	    (h₁₂ : Exact f₁ f₂) (h₂₃ : Exact f₂ f₃) (h₃ : Surjective f₃)
552	    (h₄ : IsWeaklyRegular M₄ rs) :
553	    Exact (mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList rs) f₁))
554	          (mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList rs) f₂)) := by
555	  induction h₄ generalizing M M₂ M₃ with
556	  | nil =>
557	    apply (Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv ?_ ?_).mp h₁₂
558	    any_goals exact quotEquivOfEqBot _ <|
559	      Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
560	    all_goals exact quot_hom_ext _ _ _ fun _ => rfl
561	  | cons r rs h₄ _ ih =>
562	    specialize ih
563	      (map_first_exact_on_four_term_exact_of_isSMulRegular_last h₁₂ h₂₃ h₄)
564	      (map_exact r h₂₃ h₃) (map_surjective r h₃)
565	    have H₁ := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality r rs f₁
566	    have H₂ := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality r rs f₂
567	    exact (Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv H₁.symm H₂.symm).mp ih

574	private lemma IsWeaklyRegular.swap {a b : R} (h1 : IsWeaklyRegular M [a, b])
575	    (h2 : torsionBy R M b = a • torsionBy R M b → torsionBy R M b = ⊥) :
576	    IsWeaklyRegular M [b, a] := by
577	  rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, isWeaklyRegular_singleton_iff] at h1 ⊢
578	  obtain ⟨ha, hb⟩ := h1
579	  rw [← isSMulRegular_iff_torsionBy_eq_bot] at h2
580	  specialize h2 (le_antisymm ?_ (smul_le_self_of_tower a (torsionBy R M b)))
581	  · refine le_of_eq_of_le ?_ <| smul_top_inf_eq_smul_of_isSMulRegular_on_quot <|
582	      ha.of_injective _ <| ker_eq_bot.mp <| ker_liftQ_eq_bot' _ (lsmul R M b) rfl
583	    rw [← (isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_lsmul_comap_eq _ _).mp hb]
584	    exact (inf_eq_right.mpr (ker_le_comap _)).symm
585	  · rwa [ha.isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_smul_top_inf_eq_smul, inf_comm, smul_comm,
586	      ← h2.isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_smul_top_inf_eq_smul, and_iff_left hb]

588	-- TODO: Equivalence of permutability of regular sequences to regularity of
589	-- subsequences and regularity on poly ring. See [07DW] in stacks project

592	lemma IsWeaklyRegular.prototype_perm {rs : List R} (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs)
593	    {rs'} (h'' : rs ~ rs') (h' : ∀ a b rs', (a :: b :: rs') <+~ rs →
594	      let K := torsionBy R (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs' • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) b
595	      K = a • K → K = ⊥) : IsWeaklyRegular M rs' :=
596	  have H := LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr <| quotEquivOfEqBot _ <|
597	    Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
598	  (H rs').mp <| (aux [] h'' (.refl rs) (h''.symm.subperm)) <| (H rs).mpr h
599	  where aux {rs₁ rs₂} (rs₀ : List R)
600	    (h₁₂ : rs₁ ~ rs₂) (H₁ : rs₀ ++ rs₁ <+~ rs) (H₃ : rs₀ ++ rs₂ <+~ rs)
601	    (h : IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₀ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₁) :
602	    IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₀ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₂ := by {
603	  induction h₁₂ generalizing rs₀ with
604	  | nil => exact .nil R _
605	  | cons r _ ih =>
606	    let e := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopOuter M r rs₀
607	    rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, ← e.isWeaklyRegular_congr] at h ⊢
608	    refine h.imp_right (ih (r :: rs₀) ?_ ?_) <;>
609	      exact List.perm_middle.subperm_right.mp ‹_›
610	  | swap a b t =>
611	    rw [show ∀ x y z, x :: y :: z = [x, y] ++ z from fun _ _ _ => rfl,
612	      isWeaklyRegular_append_iff] at h ⊢
613	    have : Ideal.ofList [b, a] = Ideal.ofList [a, b] :=
614	      congrArg Ideal.span <| Set.ext fun _ => (List.Perm.swap a b []).mem_iff
615	    rw [(quotEquivOfEq _ _ (congrArg₂ _ this rfl)).isWeaklyRegular_congr] at h
616	    rw [List.append_cons, List.append_cons, List.append_assoc _ [b] [a]] at H₁
617	    apply (List.sublist_append_left (rs₀ ++ [b, a]) _).subperm.trans at H₁
618	    apply List.perm_append_comm.subperm.trans at H₁
619	    exact h.imp_left (swap · (h' b a rs₀ H₁))
620	  | trans h₁₂ _ ih₁₂ ih₂₃ =>
621	    have H₂ := (h₁₂.append_left rs₀).subperm_right.mp H₁
622	    exact ih₂₃ rs₀ H₂ H₃ (ih₁₂ rs₀ H₁ H₂ h) }

624	-- putting `{rs' : List R}` and `h2` after `h3` would be better for partial
625	-- application, but this argument order seems nicer overall
626	lemma IsWeaklyRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator [IsNoetherian R M]
627	    {rs rs' : List R} (h1 : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs')
628	    (h3 : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ (Module.annihilator R M).jacobson) :
629	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs' :=
630	  h1.prototype_perm h2 fun r _ _ h h' =>
631	    eq_bot_of_eq_pointwise_smul_of_mem_jacobson_annihilator
632	      (IsNoetherian.noetherian _) h'
633	      (Ideal.jacobson_mono
634	        (le_trans
635	          -- The named argument `(R := R)` below isn't necessary, but
636	          -- typechecking is much slower without it
637	          (LinearMap.annihilator_le_of_surjective (R := R) _ (mkQ_surjective _))
638	          (LinearMap.annihilator_le_of_injective _ (injective_subtype _)))
639	        (h3 r (h.subset List.mem_cons_self)))
640

647	    letI := h1.nontrivial
648	    top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator <|
649	      Ideal.span_le.mpr (h3 · <| h2.mem_iff.mpr ·)⟩

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Basis.Exact
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Cotangent.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Kaehler
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Basic
13	
14	/-!
15	# Cotangent complex of a submersive presentation
16	
17	Let `P` be a submersive presentation of `S` as an `R`-algebra and
18	denote by `I` the kernel `R[X] → S`. We show
19	
20	- `SubmersivePresentation.free_cotangent`: `I ⧸ I ^ 2` is `S`-free on the classes of `P.relation i`.
21	- `SubmersivePresentation.subsingleton_h1Cotangent`: `H¹(L_{S/R}) = 0`.
22	- `SubmersivePresentation.free_kaehlerDifferential`: `Ω[S⁄R]` is `S`-free on the images of `dxᵢ`
23	  where `i ∉ Set.range P.map`.
24	- `SubmersivePresentation.rank_kaehlerDifferential`: If `S` is non-trivial, the rank of
25	  `Ω[S⁄R]` is the dimension of `P`.
26	
27	We also provide the corresponding instances for standard smooth algebras as corollaries.
28	
29	We keep the notation `I = ker(R[X] → S)` in all docstrings of this file.
30	-/
31	
32	@[expose] public section
33	
34	namespace Algebra
35	
36	variable {R S ι σ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
37	
38	section
39	
40	open Extension Module MvPolynomial
41	
42	namespace PreSubmersivePresentation
43	
44	/--
45	Given a pre-submersive presentation, this is the composition
46	`I ⧸ I ^ 2 → ⊕ S dxᵢ → ⊕ S dxᵢ` where the second direct sum runs over
47	all `i : σ` induced by the injection `P.map : σ → ι`.
48	
49	If `P` is submersive, this is an isomorphism. See `SubmersivePresentation.cotangentEquiv`.
50	-/
51	noncomputable def cotangentComplexAux [Finite σ] (P : PreSubmersivePresentation R S ι σ) :
52	    P.toExtension.Cotangent →ₗ[S] σ → S :=
53	  Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite S S σ ∘ₗ Finsupp.lcomapDomain _ P.map_inj ∘ₗ
54	    P.cotangentSpaceBasis.repr.toLinearMap ∘ₗ P.toExtension.cotangentComplex
55	
56	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
57	lemma cotangentComplexAux_apply [Finite σ] (P : PreSubmersivePresentation R S ι σ)
58	    (x : P.ker) (i : σ) :
59	    P.cotangentComplexAux (Cotangent.mk x) i = (aeval P.val) (pderiv (P.map i) x.val) := by
60	  dsimp only [cotangentComplexAux, LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, Function.comp_apply,
61	    cotangentComplex_mk]
62	  simp only [Generators.toExtension_Ring, Finsupp.lcomapDomain_apply,
63	    Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite_apply, Finsupp.comapDomain_apply,
64	    Generators.cotangentSpaceBasis_repr_tmul, one_mul]
65	
66	lemma cotangentComplexAux_zero_iff [Finite σ] {P : PreSubmersivePresentation R S ι σ} (x : P.ker) :
67	    P.cotangentComplexAux (Cotangent.mk x) = 0 ↔
68	      ∀ i : σ, (aeval P.val) (pderiv (P.map i) x.val) = 0 := by
69	  rw [funext_iff]
70	  simp_rw [cotangentComplexAux_apply, Pi.zero_apply]
71	
72	end PreSubmersivePresentation
73	
74	namespace SubmersivePresentation
75	
76	variable [Finite σ] (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ)
77	
78	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
79	lemma cotangentComplexAux_injective : Function.Injective P.cotangentComplexAux := by
80	  rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, eq_bot_iff]
81	  intro x hx
82	  obtain ⟨(x : P.ker), rfl⟩ := Cotangent.mk_surjective x
83	  rw [Submodule.mem_bot, Cotangent.mk_eq_zero_iff]
84	  rw [LinearMap.mem_ker, P.cotangentComplexAux_zero_iff] at hx
85	  have : x.val ∈ Ideal.span (Set.range P.relation) := by
86	    rw [P.span_range_relation_eq_ker]
87	    exact x.property
88	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := Finsupp.mem_ideal_span_range_iff_exists_finsupp.mp this
89	  have heq (i : σ) :
90	      aeval P.val (pderiv (P.map i) <| c.sum fun i a ↦ a * P.relation i) = 0 := by
91	    rw [hc]
92	    apply hx
93	  simp only [Finsupp.sum, map_sum, Derivation.leibniz, smul_eq_mul, map_add, map_mul,
94	    Presentation.aeval_val_relation, zero_mul, add_zero] at heq
95	  have heq2 : ∑ i ∈ c.support,
96	      aeval P.val (c i) • (fun j ↦ aeval P.val (pderiv (P.map j) (P.relation i))) = 0 := by
97	    ext j
98	    simp only [Finset.sum_apply, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Pi.zero_apply]
99	    apply heq
100	  have (i : σ) : aeval P.val (c i) = 0 := by
101	    have := P.linearIndependent_aeval_val_pderiv_relation
102	    rw [linearIndependent_iff''] at this
103	    have := this c.support (fun i ↦ aeval P.val (c i))
104	      (by intro i; simp only [Finsupp.mem_support_iff, ne_eq, not_not]; intro h; simp [h]) heq2
105	    exact this i
106	  change _ ∈ P.ker ^ 2
107	  rw [← hc]
108	  apply Ideal.sum_mem
109	  intro i hi
110	  rw [pow_two]
111	  apply Ideal.mul_mem_mul
112	  · rw [P.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val]
113	    simpa using this i
114	  · exact P.relation_mem_ker i
115	
116	lemma cotangentComplexAux_surjective : Function.Surjective P.cotangentComplexAux := by
117	  rw [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, _root_.eq_top_iff, ← P.basisDeriv.span_eq, Submodule.span_le]
118	  rintro - ⟨i, rfl⟩
119	  use Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩
120	  ext j
121	  rw [P.cotangentComplexAux_apply]
122	  simp
123	
124	/-- The isomorphism of `S`-modules between `I ⧸ I ^ 2` and `σ → S` given
125	by `P.relation i ↦ ∂ⱼ (P.relation i)`. -/
126	@[simps! apply]
127	noncomputable def cotangentEquiv : P.toExtension.Cotangent ≃ₗ[S] σ → S :=
128	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨P.cotangentComplexAux_injective, P.cotangentComplexAux_surjective⟩
129	
130	lemma cotangentComplex_injective : Function.Injective P.toExtension.cotangentComplex := by
131	  have := P.cotangentComplexAux_injective
132	  simp only [PreSubmersivePresentation.cotangentComplexAux, LinearMap.coe_comp,
133	    LinearEquiv.coe_coe] at this
134	  exact Function.Injective.of_comp (Function.Injective.of_comp <| Function.Injective.of_comp this)
135	
136	/-- If `P` is a submersive presentation, `H¹` of the associated cotangent complex vanishes. -/
137	instance subsingleton_h1Cotangent : Subsingleton P.toExtension.H1Cotangent := by
138	  rw [Algebra.Extension.subsingleton_h1Cotangent]
139	  exact cotangentComplex_injective P
140	
141	/-- The classes of `P.relation i` form a basis of `I ⧸ I ^ 2`. -/
142	@[stacks 00T7 "(3)"]
143	noncomputable def basisCotangent : Basis σ S P.toExtension.Cotangent :=
144	  P.basisDeriv.map P.cotangentEquiv.symm
145	
146	lemma basisCotangent_apply (r : σ) :
147	    P.basisCotangent r = Extension.Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation r, P.relation_mem_ker r⟩ := by
148	  symm
149	  apply P.cotangentEquiv.injective
150	  ext
151	  simp_rw [basisCotangent, Basis.map_apply, LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply, basisDeriv_apply]
152	  apply P.toPreSubmersivePresentation.cotangentComplexAux_apply _ _
153	
154	@[stacks 00T7 "(3)"]
155	instance free_cotangent : Module.Free S P.toExtension.Cotangent :=
156	  Module.Free.of_basis P.basisCotangent
157	
158	/--
159	If `P` is a submersive presentation, this is the section of the map
160	`I ⧸ I ^ 2 → ⊕ S dxᵢ` given by projecting to the summands indexed by `σ` and composing with the
161	inverse of `P.cotangentEquiv`.
162	
163	By `SubmersivePresentation.sectionCotangent_comp` this is indeed a section.
164	-/
165	noncomputable def sectionCotangent : P.toExtension.CotangentSpace →ₗ[S] P.toExtension.Cotangent :=
166	  (cotangentEquiv P).symm ∘ₗ (Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite S S σ).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
167	    Finsupp.lcomapDomain _ P.map_inj ∘ₗ P.cotangentSpaceBasis.repr.toLinearMap
168	
169	lemma sectionCotangent_eq_iff (x : P.toExtension.CotangentSpace) (y : P.toExtension.Cotangent) :
170	    sectionCotangent P x = y ↔
171	      ∀ i : σ, P.cotangentSpaceBasis.repr x (P.map i) = (P.cotangentComplexAux y) i := by
172	  simp only [sectionCotangent, LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, Function.comp_apply]
173	  rw [← (cotangentEquiv P).injective.eq_iff, funext_iff, LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
174	  simp
175	
176	lemma sectionCotangent_comp :
177	    sectionCotangent P ∘ₗ P.toExtension.cotangentComplex = LinearMap.id := by
178	  ext : 1
179	  simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
180	  rw [sectionCotangent_eq_iff]
181	  intro i
182	  rfl
183	
184	lemma sectionCotangent_zero_of_notMem_range (i : ι) (hi : i ∉ Set.range P.map) :
185	    (sectionCotangent P) (P.cotangentSpaceBasis i) = 0 := by
186	  classical
187	  contrapose hi
188	  rw [sectionCotangent_eq_iff] at hi
189	  simp only [Basis.repr_self, map_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Finsupp.single_apply] at hi
190	  grind
191	
192	/--
193	Given a submersive presentation of `S` as `R`-algebra, any indexing type `κ` complementary to
194	the `σ` in `ι` indexes a basis of `Ω[S⁄R]`.
195	See `SubmersivePresentation.basisKaehler` for the special case `κ = (Set.range P.map)ᶜ`.
196	-/
197	noncomputable def basisKaehlerOfIsCompl {κ : Type*} {f : κ → ι}
198	    (hf : Function.Injective f) (hcompl : IsCompl (Set.range f) (Set.range P.map)) :
199	    Basis κ S Ω[S⁄R] := by
200	  apply P.cotangentSpaceBasis.ofSplitExact (sectionCotangent_comp P)
201	    Extension.exact_cotangentComplex_toKaehler Extension.toKaehler_surjective hf (b := P.map)
202	  · intro i
203	    apply sectionCotangent_zero_of_notMem_range _ _
204	    simp [← hcompl.compl_eq]
205	  · simp only [sectionCotangent, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_assoc, LinearEquiv.coe_coe]
206	    apply LinearIndependent.map' _ _ P.cotangentEquiv.symm.ker
207	    convert (Pi.basisFun S σ).linearIndependent
208	    classical
209	    ext i j
210	    simp only [Function.comp_apply, Basis.repr_self, Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite_apply,
211	      Pi.basisFun_apply]
212	    simp [Finsupp.single_eq_pi_single]
213	  · exact hcompl.2
214	
215	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
216	@[simp]
217	lemma basisKaehlerOfIsCompl_apply {κ : Type*} {f : κ → ι}
218	    (hf : Function.Injective f) (hcompl : IsCompl (Set.range f) (Set.range P.map)) (k : κ) :
219	    P.basisKaehlerOfIsCompl hf hcompl k = KaehlerDifferential.D _ _ (P.val (f k)) := by
220	  simp [basisKaehlerOfIsCompl]

325	This is a type synonym so that `P.Ring` can act on it through the action of `S` without creating
326	a diamond. -/
327	def Cotangent : Type _ := P.ker.Cotangent

329	noncomputable
330	instance : AddCommGroup P.Cotangent := inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup P.ker.Cotangent)
331	
332	variable {P}
333	
334	/-- The identity map `P.ker.Cotangent → P.Cotangent` into the type synonym. -/
335	def Cotangent.of (x : P.ker.Cotangent) : P.Cotangent := x
336	
337	/-- The identity map `P.Cotangent → P.ker.Cotangent` from the type synonym. -/
338	def Cotangent.val (x : P.Cotangent) : P.ker.Cotangent := x

340	@[ext]
341	lemma Cotangent.ext {x y : P.Cotangent} (e : x.val = y.val) : x = y := e

343	namespace Cotangent
344	
345	variable (x y : P.Cotangent) (w z : P.ker.Cotangent)
346	
347	@[simp] lemma val_add : (x + y).val = x.val + y.val := rfl
348	@[simp] lemma val_zero : (0 : P.Cotangent).val = 0 := rfl
349	@[simp] lemma of_add : of (w + z) = of w + of z := rfl
350	@[simp] lemma of_zero : (of 0 : P.Cotangent) = 0 := rfl
351	@[simp] lemma of_val : of x.val = x := rfl
352	@[simp] lemma val_of : (of w).val = w := rfl

357	lemma Cotangent.smul_eq_zero_of_mem (p : P.Ring) (hp : p ∈ P.ker) (m : P.ker.Cotangent) :
358	    p • m = 0 :=
359	  Ideal.Cotangent.smul_eq_zero_of_mem hp m

363	noncomputable
364	instance Cotangent.module : Module S P.Cotangent where
365	  smul := fun r s ↦ .of (P.σ r • s.val)
366	  smul_zero := fun r ↦ ext (smul_zero (P.σ r))
367	  smul_add := fun r x y ↦ ext (smul_add (P.σ r) x.val y.val)
368	  add_smul := fun r s x ↦ by
369	    have := smul_eq_zero_of_mem (P.σ (r + s) - (P.σ r + P.σ s) : P.Ring) (by simp) x
370	    simpa only [sub_smul, add_smul, sub_eq_zero]
371	  zero_smul := fun x ↦ smul_eq_zero_of_mem (P.σ 0 : P.Ring) (by simp) x
372	  one_smul := fun x ↦ by

377	    simpa only [sub_smul, mul_smul, sub_eq_zero] using this

    {R' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R'] [IsLocalRing R']

  have h := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs hReg

/-! ### Iter-201 Step A2 substrate (cotangent linear-independence transport)

Two axiom-clean substrate helpers needed by the iter-201+ A2 step of the
Stacks-00SW Jacobian-regular-sequence witness construction
(per `analogies/coe-stacks00sw.md`). They package the
`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent` linear-independence statement
in two forward-friendly forms:

* `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent` — the
  `S`-lin-indep version of the family `i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, _⟩`,
  obtained directly from `P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent` via
  `basisCotangent_apply`.
* `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent_localized`
  — the same family transported through the canonical localisation map
  `Cot →ₗ[S] LocalizedModule p.primeCompl Cot` at any prime ideal `p` of `S`,
  yielding `Localization.AtPrime p`-lin-indep. This composes the previous
  helper with Mathlib's `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule`.

Together these realise Analogue D of the analogist recipe (lin-indep
preservation under localisation). The remaining substantive step in the A2
chain — identifying `LocalizedModule p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent`
with `(I·A) / (I·A)²` over the localisation `A = S_p`, i.e.\ the
conormal-localisation iso (Stacks 02JK, `Algebra.Generators.Cotangent.tensor`
variant for `AtPrime` rather than `Localization.Away`) — is a separate
Mathlib gap not landed here. -/

/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 1.** For a submersive
presentation `P : R → S` of an `R`-algebra, the family
`i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩`
in `P.toExtension.Cotangent` is `S`-linearly independent.

This is a forward-ergonomics re-package of `P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent`
+ `P.basisCotangent_apply`: the basis-form lin-indep statement is restated
with the explicit `Cotangent.mk`-of-relation form on the consumer side, so
downstream callers do not need to manually unfold the basis abstraction.

Axiom-clean: 1 `Basis.linearIndependent` + 1 funext rewrite. -/
private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent
    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    {ι σ : Type*} [Finite σ]
    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ) :
    LinearIndependent S
      (fun i : σ => Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) := by
  have h := P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent
  have heq : (fun i : σ => Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) = P.basisCotangent := by
    funext i
    exact (P.basisCotangent_apply i).symm
  rw [heq]
  exact h

/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 2.** Transport of
`submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent` through the
canonical localisation map of `P.toExtension.Cotangent` at any prime `p` of
`S`. The resulting family in `LocalizedModule p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent`
is `Localization.AtPrime p`-linearly independent.

Composes the previous helper with Mathlib's
`LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule` (in
`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Module`) and the canonical
`LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap` + `LocalizedModule.isModule`-driven
`IsLocalizedModule` instance on the generic `LocalizedModule` construction.

This is Analogue D of the `coe-stacks00sw` mathlib-analogist recipe at the
level of the un-quotiented cotangent module; the residual A2 work
(identifying the localised cotangent with `(I·A)/(I·A)²` over `A = S_p`,
i.e.\ Stacks 02JK's conormal-localisation iso) remains a Mathlib gap and is
not landed here.

Axiom-clean: 1 invocation of `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule` on top
of `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent`. -/
private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent_localized
    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    {ι σ : Type*} [Finite σ]
    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ)
    (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime] :
    LinearIndependent (Localization.AtPrime p)
      (fun i : σ =>
        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent)
          (Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
            (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker))) :=
  LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule (Localization.AtPrime p) p.primeCompl
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent)
    (submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent P)

/-- **Stage 6 sub-gap (i) resolution (iter-198).** Axiom-clean substrate helper:

`ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence` (regular-sequence
dimension drop), specialised to the polynomial-ring setting at a maximal
ideal. It gives the cleanest forward-compatible Step 3 "endpoint":
once the Step A `IsRegular` witness lands axiom-clean upstream (per the
`coe-stacks00sw` analogist recipe), this lemma reduces the
`ringKrullDim Sₘ = n` conclusion of Stacks 00OE to a one-line invocation.

    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ix : Type v} [Finite ix]
    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ix k)) [m.IsMaximal]
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ix k) A]
    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] [IsNoetherianRing A]
    (rs : List A) (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A rs)
    {b : ℕ} (hLen : rs.length = b) :
    ringKrullDim (A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) =
      (Nat.card ix : WithBot ℕ∞) := by

  exact ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence rs hReg
    (ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial m A) hLen
